cards.name: Scatterbug

abilities.name: Adaptive Evolution
abilities.text: This Pokémon can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon scatters poisonous powder to repel enemies. It will eat different plants depending on where it lives.

cards.name: Pineco

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: It spits out a fluid that it uses to glue tree bark to its body. The fluid hardens when it touches air.

cards.name: Cacturne

abilities.name: Counterattack Quills
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Packs of them follow travelers through the desert until the travelers can no longer move.

cards.name: Gogoat

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It can sense the feelings of others by touching them with its horns. This species has assisted people with their work since 5,000 years ago.

cards.name: Sprigatito
cards.flavorText: Its fluffy fur is similar in composition to plants. This Pokémon frequently washes its face to keep it from drying out.

cards.name: Meowscarada

attacks.name: Trick Cape
attacks.text: You may put an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon into their hand.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon uses the reflective fur lining its cape to camouflage the stem of its flower, creating the illusion that the flower is floating.

cards.name: Tarountula

attacks.name: String Haul
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.

cards.flavorText: The ball of threads wrapped around its body is elastic enough to deflect the scythes of Scyther, this Pokémon's natural enemy.

cards.name: Tarountula

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: The thread it secretes from its rear is as strong as wire. The secret behind the thread's strength is the topic of ongoing research.

cards.name: Smoliv
cards.flavorText: It protects itself from enemies by emitting oil from the fruit on its head. This oil is bitter and astringent enough to make someone flinch.

cards.name: Smoliv

attacks.name: Nutrients
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It protects itself from enemies by emitting oil from the fruit on its head. This oil is bitter and astringent enough to make someone flinch.

cards.name: Spidops ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Trap Territory
abilities.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost is Colorless more.

attacks.name: Wire Hang
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

cards.flavorText: The thread it secretes from its rear is as strong as wire. The secret behind the thread's strength is the topic of ongoing research.

cards.name: Arboliva

abilities.name: Enriching Oil
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may heal all damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This calm Pokémon is very compassionate. It will share its delicious, nutrient-rich oil with weakened Pokémon.

cards.name: Toedscool

attacks.name: Furious Kicks
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: Though it looks like Tentacool, Toedscool is a completely different species. Its legs may be thin, but it can run at a speed of 30 mph.

cards.name: Heracross

attacks.name: Superpowered Throw
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

cards.flavorText: With its herculean powers, it can easily throw around an object that is 100 times its own weight.

cards.name: Breloom
cards.flavorText: It scatters poisonous spores and throws powerful punches while its foe is hampered by inhaled spores.

cards.name: Tropius

attacks.name: Fresh-Picked Fruit
attacks.text: Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It lives in tropical jungles. The bunch of fruit around its neck is delicious. The fruit grows twice a year.

cards.name: Vivillon

attacks.name: Miracle Powder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a Special Condition. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon was born in a land where flowers bloom. It scatters colorful, toxic scales from its wings during battle.

cards.name: Skiddo
cards.flavorText: Until recently, people living in the mountains would ride on the back of these Pokémon to traverse the mountain paths.

cards.name: Floragato
cards.flavorText: Floragato deftly wields the vine hidden beneath its long fur, slamming the hard flower bud against its opponents.

cards.name: Toedscruel

attacks.name: Eerie Tentacles
attacks.text: You may move an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon to 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Triple Smash
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: It coils its 10 tentacles around prey and sucks out their nutrients, causing the prey pain. The folds along the rim of its head are a popular delicacy.

cards.name: Shroomish

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It prefers damp places. By day it remains still in the forest shade. It releases toxic powder from its head.

cards.name: Cacnea

abilities.name: Counterattack Quills
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It prefers harsh environments, such as deserts. It can survive for 30 days on water stored in its body.

cards.name: Spewpa

abilities.name: Adaptive Evolution
abilities.text: This Pokémon can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.

cards.flavorText: Spewpa doesn't live in a fixed location. It roams where it pleases across the fields and mountains, building up the energy it needs to evolve.

cards.name: Tarountula

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: The thread it secretes from its rear is as strong as wire. The secret behind the thread's strength is the topic of ongoing research.

cards.name: Dolliv

attacks.name: Apply Oil
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

cards.flavorText: Dolliv shares its tasty, fresh-scented oil with others. This species has coexisted with humans since times long gone.

cards.name: Toedscool

attacks.name: Spore
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

cards.flavorText: Though it looks like Tentacool, Toedscool is a completely different species. Its legs may be thin, but it can run at a speed of 30 mph.

cards.name: Gyarados ex
rules: Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Tyrannical Tail
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 180 more damage.

cards.flavorText: An underpowered, pathetic Pokémon. It may jump high on rare occasions but never more than seven feet.

cards.name: Scovillain

attacks.name: Hot Bite
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Super Spicy Breath
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Fire Energy attached, this attack does 90 more damage.

cards.flavorText: The green head has turned vicious due to the spicy chemicals stimulating its brain. Once it goes on a rampage, there is no stopping it.

cards.name: Bruxish

attacks.name: Vivid Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: It grinds its teeth with great force to stimulate its brain. It fires the psychic energy created by this process from the protuberance on its head.

cards.name: Wiglett

attacks.name: Dig a Little
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.

cards.name: Crocalor
cards.flavorText: The valve in Crocalor's flame sac is closely connected to its vocal cords. This Pokémon utters a guttural cry as it spews flames every which way.

cards.name: Wugtrio

attacks.name: Undersea Tunnel
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, discard the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck.

cards.flavorText: It has a vicious temperament, contrary to what its appearance may suggest. It wraps its long bodies around prey, then drags the prey into its den.

cards.name: Skeledirge

attacks.name: Passionate Singing
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Blazing Shout
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: Skeledirge's gentle singing soothes the souls of all that hear it. It burns its enemies to a crisp with flames of over 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit.

cards.name: Cetoddle
cards.flavorText: It lives in frigid regions in pods of five or so individuals. It loves the minerals found in snow and ice.

cards.name: Charcadet
cards.flavorText: Burnt charcoal came to life and became a Pokémon. Possessing a fiery fighting spirit, Charcadet will battle even tough opponents.

cards.name: Buizel
cards.flavorText: It spins its two tails like a screw to propel itself through water. The tails also slice clinging seaweed.

cards.name: Cetitan

attacks.name: Sweeping Tackle
attacks.text: This attack does 20 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Ice energy builds up in the horn on its upper jaw, causing the horn to reach cryogenic temperatures that freeze its surroundings.

cards.name: Clauncher
cards.flavorText: Clauncher's claws will regrow if they fall off. The meat inside the claws is edible, but it has a distinct flavor that doesn't appeal to all tastes.

cards.name: Clawitzer

attacks.name: Aqua Cannon
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

cards.flavorText: The cannonballs of seawater that Clawitzer launches from its claw are powerful enough to punch through tanker hulls.

cards.name: Quaxwell
cards.flavorText: These Pokémon constantly run through shallow waters to train their legs, then compete with each other to see which of them kicks most gracefully.

cards.name: Quaquaval

abilities.name: Energy Carnival
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: A single kick from a Quaquaval can send a truck rolling. This Pokémon uses its powerful legs to perform striking dances from far-off lands.

cards.name: Wiglett

attacks.name: Twisting Strike
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.

cards.name: Capsakid

attacks.name: Slightly Spicy
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

cards.flavorText: Traditional Paldean dishes can be extremely spicy because they include the shed front teeth of Capsakid among their ingredients.

cards.name: Growlithe

attacks.name: Relentless Flames
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: It's very friendly and faithful to people. It will try to repel enemies by barking and biting.

cards.name: Capsakid

attacks.name: Increasing Spice
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Fire Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: Traditional Paldean dishes can be extremely spicy because they include the shed front teeth of Capsakid among their ingredients.

cards.name: Arcanine ex
rules: Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Raging Claws
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bright Flame
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fire Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It's very friendly and faithful to people. It will try to repel enemies by barking and biting.

cards.name: Torkoal

attacks.name: Concentrated Fire
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: It burns coal inside its shell for energy. It blows out black soot if it is endangered.

cards.name: Fuecoco
cards.flavorText: Its flame sac is small, so energy is always leaking out. This energy is released from the dent atop Fuecoco's head and flickers to and fro.

cards.name: Charcadet

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Burnt charcoal came to life and became a Pokémon. Possessing a fiery fighting spirit, Charcadet will battle even tough opponents.

cards.name: Slowbro

abilities.name: Strange Behavior
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your other Pokémon to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bubble Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: If the tail-biting Shellder is thrown off in a harsh battle, this Pokémon reverts to being an ordinary Slowpoke.

cards.name: Floatzel

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: With its flotation sac inflated, it can carry people on its back. It deflates the sac before it dives.

cards.name: Alomomola

attacks.name: Aqua Slash
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

cards.flavorText: It gently holds injured and weak Pokémon in its fins. Its special membrane heals their wounds.

cards.name: Growlithe

attacks.name: Stoke
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Fire Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: It's very friendly and faithful to people. It will try to repel enemies by barking and biting.

cards.name: Houndour
cards.flavorText: It uses different kinds of cries for communicating with others of its kind and for pursuing its prey.

cards.name: Houndoom

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Upon hearing its eerie howls, other Pokémon get the shivers and head straight back to their nests.

cards.name: Armarouge

abilities.name: Fire Off
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may move a Fire Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flame Cannon
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

cards.flavorText: Armarouge evolved through the use of a set of armor that belonged to a distinguished warrior. This Pokémon is incredibly loyal.

cards.name: Slowpoke

attacks.name: Rest
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Asleep. Heal 30 damage from it.

cards.flavorText: It is always vacantly lost in thought, but no one knows what it is thinking about. It is good at fishing with its tail.

cards.name: Magikarp

attacks.name: Jump
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: An underpowered, pathetic Pokémon. It may jump high on rare occasions but never more than seven feet.

cards.name: Quaxly
cards.flavorText: This Pokémon migrated to Paldea from distant lands long ago. The gel secreted by its feathers repels water and grime.

cards.name: Cetoddle
cards.flavorText: It lives in frigid regions in pods of five or so individuals. It loves the minerals found in snow and ice.

cards.name: Dondozo

attacks.name: Release Rage
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each Tatsugiri in your discard pile.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon is a glutton, but it's bad at getting food. It teams up with a Tatsugiri to catch prey.

cards.name: Magnemite

attacks.name: Magnetic Switch
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It moves while constantly hovering. It discharges electromagnetic waves and so on from the units at its sides.

cards.name: Magneton

attacks.name: Explosion
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 90 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: They're formed by several Magnemite linked together. They frequently appear when sunspots flare up.

cards.name: Tatsugiri

attacks.name: Mise en Place
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Water Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Basic Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Curl Up
attacks.text: Put this Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.

cards.flavorText: This is a small dragon Pokémon. It lives inside the mouth of Dondozo to protect itself from enemies on the outside.

cards.name: Rotom

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: Research continues on this Pokémon, which could be the power source of a unique motor.

cards.name: Mareep
cards.flavorText: Its fleece grows continually. In the summer, the fleece is fully shed, but it grows back in a week.

cards.name: Pawmot

abilities.name: Electrogenesis
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Basic Lightning Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Electro Paws
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Pawmot's fluffy fur acts as a battery. It can store the same amount of electricity as an electric car.

cards.name: Pawmo

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: Pawmo uses a unique fighting technique in which it uses its forepaws to strike foes and zap them with electricity from its paw pads simultaneously.

cards.name: Miraidon

attacks.name: Lightning Laser
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: Much remains unknown about this creature. It resembles Cyclizar, but it is far more ruthless and powerful.

cards.name: Wattrel

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

cards.flavorText: When its wings catch the wind, the bones within produce electricity. This Pokémon dives into the ocean, catching prey by electrocuting them.

cards.name: Miraidon ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Tandem Unit
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 Basic Lightning Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Photon Blaster
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

cards.flavorText: Much remains unknown about this creature. It resembles Cyclizar, but it is far more ruthless and powerful.

cards.name: Wattrel
cards.flavorText: When its wings catch the wind, the bones within produce electricity. This Pokémon dives into the ocean, catching prey by electrocuting them.

cards.name: Hypno

attacks.name: Pendulum Influence
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose an attack from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon in play and use it as this attack.

cards.flavorText: Always holding a pendulum that it swings at a steady rhythm, it causes drowsiness in anyone nearby.

cards.name: Kilowattrel

attacks.name: Skill Dive
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Thunder Blast
attacks.text: Discard a Lightning Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Kilowattrel inflates its throat sac to amplify its electricity. By riding the wind, this Pokémon can fly over 430 miles in a day.

cards.name: Gardevoir ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Psychic Embrace
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Basic Psychic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Psychic Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon. You can't use this Ability on a Pokémon that would be Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Miracle Force
attacks.text: This Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.

cards.flavorText: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

cards.name: Dedenne

attacks.name: Energy Munch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It's small and its electricity-generating organ is not fully developed, so it uses its tail to absorb electricity from people's homes and charge itself.

cards.name: Drowzee

attacks.name: Forced Sleep
attacks.text: Your opponent chooses 1 of their Benched Pokémon and switches it with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

cards.flavorText: When it twitches its nose, it can tell where someone is sleeping and what that person is dreaming about.

cards.name: Klefki

abilities.name: Mischievous Lock
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, Basic Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) have no Abilities, except for Mischievous Lock.

attacks.name: Joust
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: In the past, noble families entrusted their vault keys to a Klefki. They passed the Klefki down through the generations, taking good care of it.

cards.name: Ralts
cards.flavorText: The horns on its head provide a strong power that enables it to sense people's emotions.

cards.name: Fidough
cards.flavorText: This Pokémon is smooth and moist to the touch. Yeast in Fidough's breath induces fermentation in the Pokémon's vicinity.

cards.name: Kirlia

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

cards.name: Fidough

attacks.name: Springy
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon is smooth and moist to the touch. Yeast in Fidough's breath induces fermentation in the Pokémon's vicinity.

cards.name: Shuppet

attacks.name: Enveloping Shadow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.

cards.flavorText: It feeds on the dark emotions of sadness and hatred, which make it grow steadily stronger.

cards.name: Flittle
cards.flavorText: Flittle's toes levitate about half an inch above the ground because of the psychic power emitted from the frills on the Pokémon's belly.

cards.name: Drifloon

attacks.name: Balloon Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It is whispered that any child who mistakes Drifloon for a balloon and holds on to it could wind up missing.

cards.name: Drifblim

attacks.name: Curse Spreading
attacks.text: Put 8 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

cards.flavorText: It can generate and release gas within its body. That's how it can control the altitude of its drift.

cards.name: Flabébé
cards.flavorText: This Flabébé rides a red flower. Immediately after birth, this Pokémon begins flying around in search of a flower it likes.

cards.name: Magnezone ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Energy Crush
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Pulse Launcher
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: They're formed by several Magnemite linked together. They frequently appear when sunspots flare up.

cards.name: Flaaffy

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: Because of its rubbery, electricity-resistant skin, it can store lots of electricity in its fur.

cards.name: Toxel
cards.flavorText: This selfish, attention-seeking Pokémon stores poison and electricity in two different sacs inside its body.

cards.name: Toxtricity

attacks.name: Yank Away
attacks.text: Choose 2 random cards from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals those cards and shuffles them into their deck.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: The jolts of electricity it launches by violently strumming the protrusions on its chest easily exceed 15,000 volts.

cards.name: Pawmi
cards.flavorText: The pads of its paws are electricity-discharging organs. Pawmi fires electricity from its forepaws while standing unsteadily on its hind legs.

cards.name: Florges

abilities.name: Blooming Garden
abilities.text: Your Pokémon in play have no Weakness.

attacks.name: Moonblast
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: They say that flower gardens created by Florges are constantly showered with a power that can heal both body and spirit.

cards.name: Flittle
cards.flavorText: Flittle's toes levitate about half an inch above the ground because of the psychic power emitted from the frills on the Pokémon's belly.

cards.name: Pachirisu

abilities.name: Electricity Pouches
abilities.text: This Pokémon can't be Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Everyone Discharge
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Lightning Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness.

cards.flavorText: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

cards.name: Rotom

attacks.name: Junk Hunt
attacks.text: Put an Item card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: Research continues on this Pokémon, which could be the power source of a unique motor.

cards.name: Pawmi

attacks.name: Jolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: The pads of its paws are electricity-discharging organs. Pawmi fires electricity from its forepaws while standing unsteadily on its hind legs.

cards.name: Banette ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Everlasting Darkness
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.

attacks.name: Poltergeist
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 60 damage for each Trainer card you find there.

cards.flavorText: It feeds on the dark emotions of sadness and hatred, which make it grow steadily stronger.

cards.name: Floette

attacks.name: Magical Leaf
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage, and heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon uses red wavelengths of light to pour its own energy into flowers and draw forth their latent potential.

cards.name: Dedenne

attacks.name: Second Bite
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It's small and its electricity-generating organ is not fully developed, so it uses its tail to absorb electricity from people's homes and charge itself.

cards.name: Dachsbun

abilities.name: Well-Baked Body
abilities.text: This Pokémon can't be Burned. Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Fire Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: The pleasant aroma that emanates from this Pokémon's body helps wheat grow, so Dachsbun has been treasured by farming villages.

cards.name: Flittle

attacks.name: Dash Off
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Flittle's toes levitate about half an inch above the ground because of the psychic power emitted from the frills on the Pokémon's belly.

cards.name: Greavard

attacks.name: Underworld Stroll
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a Supporter card you find there and put it on the bottom of their deck.

cards.flavorText: This friendly Pokémon doesn't like being alone. Pay it even the slightest bit of attention, and it will follow you forever.

cards.name: Greavard

attacks.name: Graveyard Gamboling
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage for each Psychic Pokémon in your discard pile.

cards.flavorText: This friendly Pokémon doesn't like being alone. Pay it even the slightest bit of attention, and it will follow you forever.

cards.name: Espathra

cards.flavorText: It immobilizes opponents by bathing them in psychic power from its large eyes. Despite its appearance, it has a vicious temperament.

cards.name: Houndstone

attacks.name: Last Respects
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each Psychic Pokémon in your discard pile.

cards.flavorText: A lovingly mourned Pokémon was reborn as Houndstone. It doesn't like anyone touching the protuberance atop its head.

cards.name: Mankey

cards.name: Primeape

cards.flavorText: Some researchers theorize that Primeape remains angry even when inside a Poké Ball.

cards.name: Annihilape

attacks.name: Dynamite Punch
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: It imbues its fists with the power of the rage that it kept hidden in its heart. Opponents struck by these imbued fists will be shattered to their core.

cards.name: Medicham

attacks.name: Acu-Punch-Ture

attacks.name: Kick Shot

cards.flavorText: Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe's next move.

cards.name: Meditite

attacks.name: Feint
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

cards.flavorText: It never skips its daily yoga training. It heightens its inner strength through meditation.

cards.name: Riolu

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: They communicate with one another using their auras. They are able to run all through the night.

cards.name: Riolu

cards.name: Lucario

cards.flavorText: It's said that no foe can remain invisible to Lucario, since it can detect auras—even those of foes it could not otherwise see.

cards.name: Sandile
cards.flavorText: It submerges itself in sand and moves as if swimming. This wise behavior keeps its enemies from finding it and maintains its temperature.

cards.name: Krookodile

attacks.name: Earthquake

cards.flavorText: After clamping down with its powerful jaws, it twists its body around to rip its prey in half.

cards.name: Hawlucha

cards.flavorText: Its elegant finishing moves—performed by nimbly leaping around using its wings—are polished in the forest where it was born and raised.

cards.name: Krokorok

attacks.name: Payback
attacks.text: If your opponent has exactly 1 Prize card remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Protected by thin membranes, their eyes can see even in the dead of night. They live in groups of a few individuals.

cards.name: Silicobra
cards.flavorText: Silicobra's neck pouch, which can inflate and deflate like a balloon, gets more elastic each time Silicobra sheds its skin.

cards.name: Sandaconda

attacks.name: Sandstorm Spray

cards.flavorText: It will expand its body as much as it can and then contract itself, blasting out sand with enough force to wash away a dump truck.

cards.name: Hoppip

attacks.name: Splashing Dodge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon is blown across vast distances by the wind. It is unclear where the Hoppip of Paldea originally came from.

cards.name: Skiploom

abilities.name: Drifting Dodge
abilities.text: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.

attacks.name: Flowery Zephyr

cards.name: Pineco
cards.flavorText: It likes to make its shell thicker by adding layers of tree bark. The additional weight doesn't bother it.

cards.name: Jumpluff

abilities.name: Drifting Dodge
abilities.text: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.

attacks.name: Fluffy Breeze
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: Jumpluff travels on seasonal winds. Once its cotton spores run out, its journey ends, as does its life.

cards.name: Tropius

cards.flavorText: It lives in tropical jungles. The bunch of fruit around its neck is delicious. The fruit grows twice a year.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: It loves sweet nectar. To keep all the nectar to itself, it hurls rivals away with its prized horn.

cards.flavorText: Floragato deftly wields the vine hidden beneath its long fur, slamming the hard flower bud against its opponents.

cards.flavorText: It houses its colony in cells in its body and releases various pheromones to make those grubs do its bidding.

cards.name: Snover
cards.flavorText: During cold seasons, it migrates to the mountain's lower reaches. It returns to the snow-covered summit in the spring.

cards.name: Abomasnow

abilities.name: Freezing Disaster

cards.flavorText: It lives a quiet life on mountains that are perpetually covered in snow. It hides itself by whipping up blizzards.

cards.name: Nymble

attacks.name: Blot

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon creates a mud ball by mixing sand and dirt with psychic energy. It treasures its mud ball more than its own life.

cards.name: Charcadet

cards.name: Gyarados

attacks.name: Revengeful Storm

attacks.name: Water Pulse

attacks.name: Singe

attacks.name: Overrun
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: It carries food all day long. There are tales about lost people who were saved by the food it had.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 90 more damage.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon is incredibly popular, possibly because its passionate dancing is a great match with the temperament of Paldean people.

attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon migrated to Paldea from distant lands long ago. The gel secreted by its feathers repels water and grime.

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: This species left the ocean and began living on land a very long time ago. It seems to be closely related to Wailmer.

cards.name: Arctibax

attacks.name: Growl

cards.name: Shinx

cards.name: Wattrel

attacks.name: Devastating Wind

cards.flavorText: When its wings catch the wind, the bones within produce electricity. This Pokémon dives into the ocean, catching prey by electrocuting them.

cards.flavorText: Its constant mischief and misdeeds resulted in it being bound to an Odd Keystone by a mysterious spell.

cards.name: Gothitelle

abilities.name: Read the Stars

attacks.name: Psych Out

cards.flavorText: Gothitelle unleashes psychic energy and shows opponents dreams of the universe's end. These dreams are apparently ethereal and beautiful.

cards.name: Tinkatuff

attacks.name: Boundless Power

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon will attack groups of Pawniard and Bisharp, gathering metal from them in order to create a large and sturdy hammer.

cards.name: Tinkatuff

attacks.name: Play Rough

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon will attack groups of Pawniard and Bisharp, gathering metal from them in order to create a large and sturdy hammer.

cards.name: Mankey

cards.name: Pincurchin

cards.flavorText: When its group is attacked, Pawmo is the first to leap into battle, defeating enemies with a fighting technique that utilizes electric shocks.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon normally is slow to react, but once it enters battle, it will strike down its enemies with lightning-fast movements.

cards.name: Tadbulb
cards.flavorText: Tadbulb shakes its tail to generate electricity. If it senses danger, it will make its head blink on and off to alert its allies.

cards.name: Jigglypuff

attacks.name: Sing

cards.flavorText: When its huge eyes waver, it sings a mysteriously soothing melody that lulls its enemies to sleep.

cards.flavorText: It has a very fine fur. Take care not to make it angry, or it may inflate steadily and hit with a body slam.

cards.name: Misdreavus

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon startles people in the middle of the night. It gathers fear as its energy.

cards.name: Gothorita
