attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon will hypnotize children to put them to sleep before carrying them away. Be wary of nights when the starlight is bright.

cards.name: Dedenne ex
rules: Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Tail Swap
attacks.text: Move all damage counters from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Wondrous Shot
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.name: Mimikyu

abilities.name: Safeguard
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon ex and Pokémon V.

attacks.name: Ghost Eye
attacks.text: Put 7 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon lives in dark places untouched by sunlight. When it appears before humans, it hides itself under a cloth that resembles a Pikachu.

cards.name: Primeape

attacks.name: Pummel
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It becomes wildly furious if it even senses someone looking at it. It chases anyone that meets its glare.

cards.name: Sudowoodo

attacks.name: Hit and Hide
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Although it always pretends to be a tree, its composition appears more similar to rock than to vegetation.

cards.name: Larvitar

attacks.name: Double Stab
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: Born deep underground, this Pokémon becomes a pupa after eating enough dirt to make a mountain.

cards.name: Makuhita
cards.flavorText: It grows stronger by enduring harsh training. It is a gutsy Pokémon that can withstand any attack.

cards.name: Voltorb
cards.flavorText: It rolls to move. If the ground is uneven, a sudden jolt from hitting a bump can cause it to explode.

cards.name: Electrode
cards.flavorText: The more energy it charges up, the faster it gets. But this also makes it more likely to explode.

cards.name: Bellibolt ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Jumping Press
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Paralyzing Ball
attacks.text: You may discard 2 Lightning Energy from this Pokémon to make your opponent's Active Pokémon Paralyzed.

cards.name: Kilowattrel

attacks.name: Jet Wing
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

cards.flavorText: Kilowattrel inflates its throat sac to amplify its electricity. By riding the wind, this Pokémon can fly over 430 miles in a day.

cards.name: Slowking ex
rules: Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Profound Knowledge
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Wise Headbutt
attacks.text: You may search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.name: Gothita

attacks.name: Eerie Wave
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon is normally very innocent. When it is staring at something invisible, it is unblinking and utterly silent.

cards.name: Oranguru

attacks.name: Plotter's Command
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't use that attack.

cards.flavorText: People used to mistake Oranguru for a human when they saw it issue command after command to the other Pokémon of the forest.

cards.name: Sandygast
cards.flavorText: If it loses its shovel, it will stick something else— like a branch—in its head to make do until it finds another shovel.

cards.name: Tinkatink

attacks.name: Scrap Pickup
attacks.text: Put an Item card from your discard pile into your hand.

cards.flavorText: It swings its handmade hammer around to protect itself, but the hammer is often stolen by Pokémon that eat metal.

cards.name: Tinkatink

attacks.name: Brutal Swing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It swings its handmade hammer around to protect itself, but the hammer is often stolen by Pokémon that eat metal.

cards.name: Tinkaton

abilities.name: Gather Materials
abilities.text: You must discard a card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Special Hammer
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, this attack does 90 more damage.

cards.flavorText: This intelligent Pokémon has a very daring disposition. It knocks rocks into the sky with its hammer, aiming for flying Corviknight.

cards.name: Paldean Tauros

attacks.name: Raging Horns
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Combat Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: This kind of Tauros, known as the Combat Breed, is distinguished by its thick, powerful muscles and its fierce disposition.

cards.name: Pupitar
cards.flavorText: This pupa flies around wildly by venting with great force the gas pressurized inside its body.

cards.name: Shinx

abilities.name: Big Roar
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: The extension and contraction of its muscles generates electricity. It glows when in trouble.

cards.name: Luxio
cards.flavorText: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

cards.name: Luxray

abilities.name: Swelling Flash
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in your hand and you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, you may put this Pokémon onto your Bench.

attacks.name: Wild Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: It can see clearly through walls to track down its prey and seek its lost young.

cards.name: Pincurchin

attacks.name: Needle Crush
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon generates electricity when it digests food. It uses its five hard teeth to scrape seaweed off surfaces and eat it.

cards.name: Wattrel

attacks.name: United Wings
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the United Wings attack.

cards.flavorText: When its wings catch the wind, the bones within produce electricity. This Pokémon dives into the ocean, catching prey by electrocuting them.

cards.name: Slowpoke

attacks.name: Tail-Fishing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. If tails, discard a card from your hand.

cards.flavorText: It is incredibly slow and dopey. It takes five seconds for it to feel pain when under attack.

cards.name: Mismagius

abilities.name: Magical Flick
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may move an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon to 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Its cry sounds like an incantation. It is said the cry may rarely be imbued with happiness-giving power.

cards.name: Palossand

attacks.name: Earthen Power
attacks.text: If you have a Stadium in play, this attack does 80 more damage.

cards.flavorText: The terrifying Palossand drags smaller Pokémon into its sandy body. Once its victims are trapped, it drains them of their vitality whenever it pleases.

cards.name: Ceruledge

attacks.name: Life Sucker
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fighting Sword
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 100 more damage.

cards.flavorText: The fiery blades on its arms burn fiercely with the lingering resentment of a sword wielder who fell before accomplishing their goal.

cards.name: Rabsca

attacks.name: Revival Blessing
attacks.text: Put a Pokémon from your discard pile onto your Bench.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

cards.flavorText: The body that supports the ball barely moves. Therefore, it is thought that the true body of this Pokémon is actually inside the ball.

cards.name: Tinkatink

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

cards.flavorText: It swings its handmade hammer around to protect itself, but the hammer is often stolen by Pokémon that eat metal.

cards.name: Lycanroc ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Scary Fangs
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if it is Knocked Out), put 10 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

cards.name: Rockruff
cards.flavorText: This Pokémon is very friendly when it's young. Its disposition becomes vicious once it matures, but it never forgets the kindness of its master.

cards.name: Hariyama

abilities.name: Arm Thrust Practice
abilities.text: All of your Pokémon take 10 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: It loves challenging others to tests of strength. It has the power to stop a train with a slap.

cards.name: Croagunk

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

cards.flavorText: Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

cards.name: Toxicroak

attacks.name: Knuckle Claws
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

cards.flavorText: Swaying and dodging the attacks of its foes, it weaves its flexible body in close, then lunges out with its poisonous claws.

cards.name: Passimian

attacks.name: Make the Assist
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon battles by throwing hard berries. It won't obey a Trainer who throws Poké Balls without skill.

cards.name: Falinks

attacks.name: Reckless Charge Together
attacks.text: If Falinks is on your Bench, this attack does 90 more damage.

cards.flavorText: The brass, which is the one that stands at the front and issues orders, is the strongest and smartest of the six.

cards.name: Nacli
cards.flavorText: It was born in a layer of rock salt deep under the earth. This species was particularly treasured in the old days, as they would share precious salt.

cards.name: Surskit

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: They usually live on ponds, but after an evening shower, they may appear on puddles in towns.

cards.name: Gloom

attacks.name: Inviting Scent
attacks.text: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.

cards.flavorText: What appears to be drool is actually sweet honey. It is very sticky and clings stubbornly if touched.

cards.name: Oddish

attacks.name: Feelin' Fine
attacks.text: Draw a card.

cards.flavorText: During the day, it stays in the cold underground to avoid the sun. It grows by bathing in moonlight.

cards.name: Phantump
cards.flavorText: With a voice like a human child's, it cries out to lure adults deep into the forest, getting them lost among the trees.

cards.name: Steenee

attacks.name: Aromatherapy
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Steenee spreads a sweet scent that makes others feel invigorated. This same scent is popular for antiperspirants.

cards.name: Charmeleon

attacks.name: Heat Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: If it becomes agitated during battle, it spouts intense flames, incinerating its surroundings.

cards.name: Camerupt

attacks.name: Eruption
attacks.text: Discard the top card of each player's deck. This attack does 100 more damage for each Energy card discarded in this way.

cards.flavorText: It lives in the crater of a volcano. It is well known that the humps on its back erupt every 10 years.

cards.name: Victini ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Strafe
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Victory Flame
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

cards.name: Darmanitan

attacks.name: Damage Counterpunch
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon's power level rises along with the temperature of its fire, which can reach 2,500 degrees Fahrenheit.

cards.name: Scyther

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It slashes through grass with its sharp scythes, moving too fast for the human eye to track.

cards.name: Foongus

attacks.name: Enticing Pattern
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Grass Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: There is a theory that the developer of the modern-day Poké Ball really liked Foongus, but this has not been confirmed.

cards.name: Capsakid

attacks.name: Double Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: The more sunlight this Pokémon bathes in, the more spicy chemicals are produced by its body, and thus the spicier its moves become.

cards.name: Scovillain

abilities.name: Double Type
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in play, it is Grass and Fire type.

attacks.name: Spicy Headbutt
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

cards.flavorText: The red head converts spicy chemicals into fire energy and blasts the surrounding area with a super spicy stream of flame.

cards.name: Numel

attacks.name: Hot Magma
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

cards.flavorText: Magma of almost 2,200 degrees Fahrenheit courses through its body. When it grows cold, the magma hardens and slows it.

cards.name: Darumaka

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: This popular symbol of good fortune will never fall over in its sleep, no matter how it's pushed or pulled.

cards.name: Combee

attacks.name: Share
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: At night, Combee sleep in a group of about a hundred, packed closely together in a lump.

cards.name: Bellossom

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Powerful Dance
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 90 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: Bellossom gather at times and appear to dance. They say that the dance is a ritual to summon the sun.

cards.name: Shuckle

attacks.name: Berry Scent
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: The berries stored in its vaselike shell eventually become a thick, pulpy juice.

cards.name: Masquerain

attacks.name: Panic-Prompting Pattern
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

cards.flavorText: It flaps its four wings to hover and fly freely in any direction—to and fro and sideways.

cards.name: Amoonguss

attacks.name: Dangerous Spores
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.

cards.flavorText: Be wary of the poisonous spores it releases. Mushrooms resembling Amoonguss's caps will grow out of anywhere the spores touch.

cards.name: Trevenant

abilities.name: Forest Miasma
abilities.text: During Pokémon Checkup, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, put 1 damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Lock Up
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

cards.flavorText: Small roots that extend from the tips of this Pokémon's feet can tie into the trees of the forest and give Trevenant control over them.

cards.name: Decidueye ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Total Freedom
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. If this Pokémon is on the Bench, switch it with your Active Pokémon. Or, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, switch it with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hunting Arrow
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.name: Bounsweet

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: Its sweat is sweet, like syrup made from boiled-down fruit. Because of this, Bounsweet was highly valued in the past, when sweeteners were scarce.

cards.name: Tsareena

attacks.name: Queenly Heel
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, Pokémon can't be played from your opponent's hand to evolve the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Spinning Kick
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon is proud and aggressive. However, it is said that a Tsareena will instantly become calm if someone touches the crown on its calyx.

cards.name: Smoliv

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It protects itself from enemies by emitting oil from the fruit on its head. This oil is bitter and astringent enough to make someone flinch.

cards.name: Dolliv

attacks.name: Sunny Wind
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Dolliv shares its tasty, fresh-scented oil with others. This species has coexisted with humans since times long gone.

cards.name: Toedscruel ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Protective Mycelium
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent's Pokémon done to all of your Pokémon that have Energy attached. (Existing effects are not removed. Damage is not an effect.)

attacks.name: Colony Rush
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has any Grass Energy attached.

cards.name: Vulpix

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

cards.flavorText: As each tail grows, its fur becomes more lustrous. When held, it feels slightly warm.

cards.name: Rowlet
cards.flavorText: It feels relaxed in tight, dark places and has been known to use its Trainer's pocket or bag as a nest.

cards.name: Dartrix

attacks.name: Shoot Through
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: Supremely sensitive to the presence of others, it can detect opponents standing behind it, flinging its sharp feathers to take them out.

cards.name: Arboliva

attacks.name: Healing Fruit
attacks.text: Heal all damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: This calm Pokémon is very compassionate. It will share its delicious, nutrient-rich oil with weakened Pokémon.

cards.name: Capsakid

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: The more sunlight this Pokémon bathes in, the more spicy chemicals are produced by its body, and thus the spicier its moves become.

cards.name: Charmander

attacks.name: Heat Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: From the time it is born, a flame burns at the tip of its tail. Its life would end if the flame were to go out.

cards.name: Ninetales

attacks.name: Nine-Tailed Dance
attacks.text: Put 9 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

cards.flavorText: Very smart and very vengeful. Grabbing one of its many tails could result in a 1,000-year curse.

cards.name: Entei

abilities.name: Pressure
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, attacks used by your opponent's Active Pokémon do 20 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Blaze Ball
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It is said that when it roars, a volcano erupts somewhere around the globe.

cards.name: Litwick

attacks.name: Firebreathing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

cards.flavorText: The younger the life this Pokémon absorbs, the brighter and eerier the flame on its head burns.

cards.name: Eiscue ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.name: Scalding Block
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't attack.

cards.name: Lapras

attacks.name: Hail
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: Crossing icy seas is no issue for this cold-resistant Pokémon. Its smooth skin is a little cool to the touch.

cards.name: Buizel

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It spins its two tails like a screw to propel itself through water. The tails also slice clinging seaweed.

cards.name: Beartic

attacks.name: Frost Purge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It is a ferocious, carnivorous Pokémon. Once it captures its prey, it will breathe cold air onto the prey to freeze and preserve it.

cards.name: Wiglett
cards.flavorText: This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.

cards.name: Finizen

attacks.name: Valiant Evolution
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: It likes playing with others of its kind using the water ring on its tail. It uses ultrasonic waves to sense the emotions of other living creatures.

cards.name: Palafin

attacks.name: Jet Punch
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Justice Kick
attacks.text: If this Pokémon didn't move from the Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon's ancient genes have awakened. It is now so extraordinarily strong that it can easily lift a cruise ship with one fin.

cards.name: Magneton
cards.flavorText: Three Magnemite are linked by a strong magnetic force. Earaches will occur if you get too close.

cards.name: Magnezone

attacks.name: Magnetic Repulsion
attacks.text: You may switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: As it zooms through the sky, this Pokémon seems to be receiving signals of unknown origin while transmitting signals of unknown purpose.

cards.name: Thundurus

abilities.name: Adverse Weather
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, prevent all damage done to your Benched Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gigantic Bolt
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 90 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: As it flies around, it shoots lightning all over the place and causes forest fires. It is therefore disliked.

cards.name: Toxel

attacks.name: Slight Intrusion
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: It has no problem drinking dirty water. An organ inside Toxel's body filters such water into a poisonous liquid that is harmless to Toxel.

cards.name: Tadbulb

attacks.name: Energize
attacks.text: Attach a Basic Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Tadbulb shakes its tail to generate electricity. If it senses danger, it will make its head blink on and off to alert its allies.

cards.name: Clefable ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Lunar Zone
abilities.text: All of your Pokémon that have Psychic Energy attached have no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Wondrous Moon
attacks.text: You may move any amount of Psychic Energy from your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.

cards.name: Espeon

attacks.name: Psychic Assault
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: The tip of its forked tail quivers when it is predicting its opponent's next move.

cards.name: Granbull

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: Although it's popular with young people, Granbull is timid and sensitive, so it's totally incompetent as a watchdog.

cards.name: Baltoy

attacks.name: Rapid Spin
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: It was discovered in ancient ruins. While moving, it constantly spins. It stands on one foot even when asleep.

cards.name: Claydol

attacks.name: Kaboom Doll
attacks.text: Put damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon until its remaining HP is 10. If you placed any damage counters in this way, this attack also does 120 damage to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mind Bend
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

cards.flavorText: It appears to have been born from clay dolls made by ancient people. It uses telekinesis to float and move.

cards.name: Vespiquen ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.name: Healing Pheromone
attacks.text: Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Phantom Queen
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.

cards.name: Sinistea

attacks.name: Cold Tea
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: The soul of someone who died alone possessed some leftover tea. This Pokémon appears in hotels and houses.

cards.name: Diglett

attacks.name: Hole-Diggin' Noggin
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your deck.

cards.flavorText: It lives about one yard underground, where it feeds on plant roots. It sometimes appears aboveground.

cards.name: Barboach

attacks.name: Hide
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Its two whiskers provide a sensitive radar. Even in muddy waters, it can detect its prey's location.

cards.name: Whiscash

attacks.name: Raging and Rocking
attacks.text: For each Fighting Energy attached to this Pokémon, discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

cards.flavorText: It is extremely protective of its territory. If any foe approaches, it attacks using vicious tremors.

cards.name: Crabrawler
cards.flavorText: This Pokémon punches trees and eats the berries that drop down, training itself and getting food at the same time.

cards.name: Crabominable

attacks.name: Knuckle Impact
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

cards.flavorText: The detached pincers of these Pokémon are delicious. Some Trainers bring Lechonk into the mountains just to search for them.

cards.name: Rockruff
cards.flavorText: This Pokémon is very friendly when it's young. Its disposition becomes vicious once it matures, but it never forgets the kindness of its master.

cards.name: Lycanroc

attacks.name: Finishing Fang
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has no damage counters on it before this attack does damage, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon uses its rocky mane to slash any who approach. It will even disobey its Trainer if it dislikes the orders it was given.

cards.name: Glimmet

attacks.name: Iron Defense
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.

cards.flavorText: It absorbs nutrients from cave walls. The petals it wears are made of crystallized poison.

cards.name: Paldean Wooper
cards.flavorText: After losing a territorial struggle, Wooper began living on land. The Pokémon changed over time, developing a poisonous film to protect its body.

cards.name: Paldean Clodsire

attacks.name: Splattering Poison
attacks.text: Both Active Pokémon are now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 120 more damage.

cards.flavorText: When attacked, this Pokémon will retaliate by sticking thick spines out from its body. It's a risky move that puts everything on the line.

cards.name: Heatmor

attacks.name: Energy Burner
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: A flame serves as its tongue, melting through the hard shell of Durant so that Heatmor can devour their insides.

cards.name: Floatzel

attacks.name: Swirling Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, put your opponent's Active Pokémon and all attached cards into your opponent's hand.

cards.flavorText: With its flotation sac inflated, it can carry people on its back. It deflates the sac before it dives.

cards.name: Tympole

attacks.name: Screw Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It uses sound waves to communicate with others of its kind. People and other Pokémon species can't hear its cries of warning.

cards.name: Cubchoo

attacks.name: Trip Over
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

cards.flavorText: Many of this species can be found along the shorelines of cold regions. If a Cubchoo lacks dangling snot, there's a chance it is sick.

cards.name: Cryogonal

attacks.name: First Freeze
attacks.text: If you go second and it's your first turn, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: Cryogonal appear during cold seasons. It is said that people and Pokémon who die on snowy mountains are reborn into these Pokémon.

cards.name: Magnemite
cards.flavorText: The electromagnetic waves emitted by the units at the sides of its head expel antigravity, which allows it to float.

cards.name: Tyranitar ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.name: Mountain Hurl
attacks.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.

attacks.name: Lightning Rampage
attacks.text: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 100 more damage.

cards.name: Tynamo
cards.flavorText: While one alone doesn't have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.

cards.name: Eelektrik

attacks.name: Volt Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: They coil around foes and shock them with electricity-generating organs that seem simply to be circular patterns.

cards.name: Eelektross

attacks.name: Suction Shock
attacks.text: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. If you do, this attack does 60 damage to the new Active Pokémon, and then flip a coin. If heads, that Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

cards.flavorText: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.

cards.name: Tadbulb

attacks.name: Shake and Discharge
attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: Tadbulb shakes its tail to generate electricity. If it senses danger, it will make its head blink on and off to alert its allies.

cards.name: Cleffa

attacks.name: Grasping Draw
attacks.text: Draw cards until you have 7 cards in your hand.

cards.flavorText: Because of its unusual, starlike silhouette, people believe that it came here on a meteor.

cards.name: Clefairy

attacks.name: Slap Slap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: Its adorable behavior and cry make it highly popular. However, this cute Pokémon is rarely found.

cards.name: Togepi

attacks.name: Whiny Voice
attacks.text: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

cards.flavorText: It is considered to be a symbol of good luck. Its shell is said to be filled with happiness.

cards.name: Togekiss

abilities.name: Precious Gift
abilities.text: Once at the end of your turn (after your attack), you may use this Ability. Draw cards until you have 8 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Power Cyclone
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Known as a bringer of blessings, it's been depicted on good-luck charms since ancient times.

cards.name: Snubbull

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: In contrast to its appearance, it's quite timid. When playing with other puppy Pokémon, it sometimes gets bullied.

cards.name: Lunatone

abilities.name: New Moon
abilities.text: If you have Solrock in play, prevent all effects of any Stadium done to your Pokémon in play.

cards.flavorText: It was discovered at the site of a meteor strike 40 years ago. Its stare can lull its foes to sleep.

cards.name: Greavard

attacks.name: Play Rough
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It is said that a dog Pokémon that died in the wild without ever interacting with a human was reborn as this Pokémon.

cards.name: Larvitar
cards.flavorText: Born deep underground, this Pokémon becomes a pupa after eating enough dirt to make a mountain.

cards.name: Pupitar

attacks.name: Blasting Tackle
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: This pupa flies around wildly by venting with great force the gas pressurized inside its body.

cards.name: Toedscruel

attacks.name: Double Whip
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.

cards.flavorText: These Pokémon gather into groups and form colonies deep within forests. They absolutely hate it when strangers approach.

cards.name: Koraidon ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Splitting Beam
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Gaia Press
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

cards.name: Lampent

attacks.name: Alluring Fireball
attacks.text: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. This attack does 30 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It lurks in cities, pretending to be a lamp. Once it finds someone whose death is near, it will trail quietly after them.

cards.name: Volcarona

attacks.name: Flame Cloak
attacks.text: Attach a Basic Fire Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Its burning body causes it to be unpopular in hot parts of the world, but in cold ones, Volcarona is revered as an embodiment of the sun.

cards.name: Armarouge

abilities.name: Scorching Armor
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Burned.

cards.flavorText: Armarouge evolved through the use of a set of armor that belonged to a distinguished warrior. This Pokémon is incredibly loyal.

cards.name: Sharpedo

attacks.name: Aqua Impact
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon is known as the Bully of the Sea. Any ship entering the waters Sharpedo calls home will be attacked—no exceptions.

cards.name: Seismitoad

abilities.name: Quaking Zone
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, attacks used by your opponent's Active Pokémon cost Colorless more.

attacks.name: Echoed Voice
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Echoed Voice attack does 100 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon is popular among the elderly, who say the vibrations of its lumps are great for massages.

cards.name: Toxtricity

attacks.name: Leer
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Loud Mix
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each different type of Pokémon on your Bench.

cards.flavorText: Many youths admire the way this Pokémon listlessly picks fights and keeps its cool no matter what opponent it faces.

cards.name: Bellibolt

attacks.name: Electrobullet
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.flavorText: When this Pokémon expands and contracts its wobbly body, the belly-button dynamo in its stomach produces a huge amount of electricity.

cards.name: Mawile

attacks.name: Mischievous Crunch
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Psychic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: It chomps with its gaping mouth. Its huge jaws are actually steel horns that have been transformed.

cards.name: Spoink
cards.flavorText: Spoink will die if it stops bouncing. The pearl on its head amplifies its psychic powers.

cards.name: Greavard
cards.flavorText: It is said that a dog Pokémon that died in the wild without ever interacting with a human was reborn as this Pokémon.

cards.name: Nosepass

attacks.name: Iron Collecting
attacks.text: Put up to 2 Basic Metal Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

cards.flavorText: It hunts without twitching a muscle by pulling in its prey with powerful magnetism. But sometimes it pulls natural enemies in close.

cards.name: Bonsly

attacks.name: Blubbering
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

cards.flavorText: In order to adjust the level of fluids in its body, it exudes water from its eyes. This makes it appear to be crying.

cards.name: Drilbur

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

cards.flavorText: It's a digger, using its claws to burrow through the ground. It causes damage to vegetable crops, so many farmers have little love for it.

cards.name: Stunfisk

abilities.name: Custom Trap
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, has a Pokémon Tool attached, and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 5 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rumble
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

cards.flavorText: For some reason, this Pokémon smiles slightly when it emits a strong electric current from the yellow markings on its body.

cards.name: Klawf ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Counterattacking Pincer
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), discard an Energy from the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Falling Press
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 80 more damage.

cards.name: Paldean Clodsire

attacks.name: Poison Ring
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Muddy Hammer
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

cards.flavorText: When attacked, this Pokémon will retaliate by sticking thick spines out from its body. It's a risky move that puts everything on the line.

cards.name: Chandelure

attacks.name: Combustion Chain
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: In homes illuminated by Chandelure instead of lights, funerals were a constant occurrence—or so it's said.

cards.name: Larvesta

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: This Pokémon was called the Larva That Stole the Sun. The fire Larvesta spouts from its horns can cut right through a sheet of iron.

cards.name: Charcadet
cards.flavorText: Burnt charcoal came to life and became a Pokémon. Possessing a fiery fighting spirit, Charcadet will battle even tough opponents.

cards.name: Carvanha
cards.flavorText: These Pokémon have sharp fangs and powerful jaws. Sailors avoid Carvanha dens at all costs.

cards.name: Palpitoad
cards.flavorText: On occasion, their cries are sublimely pleasing to the ear. Palpitoad with larger lumps on their bodies can sing with a wider range of sounds.

cards.name: Froakie

attacks.name: Try Bouncing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

cards.flavorText: It protects its skin by covering its body in delicate bubbles. Beneath its happy-go-lucky air, it keeps a watchful eye on its surroundings.

cards.name: Frogadier

attacks.name: Strafe
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: Its swiftness is unparalleled. It can scale a tower of more than 600 metres in a minute's time.

cards.name: Wugtrio

attacks.name: Entwining Entrapment
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

cards.flavorText: It has a vicious temperament, contrary to what its appearance may suggest. It wraps its long bodies around prey, then drags the prey into its den.

cards.name: Finizen
cards.flavorText: It likes playing with others of its kind using the water ring on its tail. It uses ultrasonic waves to sense the emotions of other living creatures.

cards.name: Pawmot ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Levin Strike
attacks.text: Discard 2 Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 220 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

cards.name: Tadbulb

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

cards.flavorText: Tadbulb shakes its tail to generate electricity. If it senses danger, it will make its head blink on and off to alert its allies.

cards.name: Bellibolt

abilities.name: Insulator
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Lightning Pokémon.

attacks.name: Thunderous Edge
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: When this Pokémon expands and contracts its wobbly body, the belly-button dynamo in its stomach produces a huge amount of electricity.

cards.name: Miraidon ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rapid Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Techno Turbo
attacks.text: Attach a Basic Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

cards.name: Togetic

attacks.name: Shared Peace
attacks.text: Each player draws 3 cards.

cards.flavorText: It grows dispirited if it is not with kind people. It can float in midair without moving its wings.

cards.name: Grumpig

attacks.name: Powerful Steps
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: It can perform odd dance steps to influence foes. Its style of dancing became hugely popular overseas.

cards.name: Solrock

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

cards.flavorText: Solar energy is the source of its power, so it is strong during the daytime. When it spins, its body shines.

cards.name: Polteageist

attacks.name: Antique Collecting
attacks.text: Put up to 2 in any combination of Item cards and Pokémon Tool cards from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Pour Tea
attacks.text: Put 5 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

cards.flavorText: The tea that composes Polteageist's body has a distinct and enjoyable flavor. Drinking too much, however, can be fatal.
